commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Slack doctor warning for name-keyed `channels.slack.channels` entries under effective allowlist policy, plus focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +57, Tests +117. Total +174 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection reproduces the issue shape: current routing ignores name-keyed Slack channel entries unless dangerous name matching is enabled, and current main has no doctor warning for those keys.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review and merge gating for the current PR head.

target.title: fix(slack): warn when channels map is keyed by name instead of channel ID
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #81665.

Under `groupPolicy: "allowlist"` (the default), a `channels.slack.channels` entry keyed by a human channel **name** instead of a Slack channel **ID** is never matched by the ID-first routing in `resolveSlackChannelConfig` (the name candidates are gated behind `allowNameMatching`, which is off under allowlist). The Zod schema accepts the shape (`z.record(z.string(), …)`) and no startup/doctor diagnostic inspects the channel-map keys, so the operator gets accepted-config + silent-runtime-drop + no signal.

## What changed

**`extensions/slack/src/doctor.ts`** — `slackDoctor.collectMutableAllowlistWarnings` now also emits a name-keyed-channel warning: for each Slack account scope where name matching is off **and** `groupPolicy` is `"allowlist"`, any `channels` map key that is not a Slack channel ID (`/^[CGD][A-Z0-9]{8,}$/`) and not the `"*"` wildcard is flagged with its config path and a re-key hint. Routing in `channel-config.ts` is untouched — this is diagnostic-only.

**`extensions/slack/src/doctor.test.ts`** — regression tests for the allowlist warning (name-keyed flagged, ID-keyed not), and the open-policy no-warn case.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `openclaw doctor` now warns when a `channels.slack.channels` map is keyed by a channel name (silently dropped under allowlist routing) instead of staying silent. Valid channel-ID keys, the `"*"` wildcard, name-matching-enabled accounts, and non-allowlist group policies are not flagged.

**Real environment tested:** Local checkout at HEAD `99ddf10cdeea3b4bfc686579b1656840248cde06` (a worktree of `origin/main`), Node v22.22.0. Drove the real exported `slackDoctor.collectMutableAllowlistWarnings` via `node --import tsx` (the actual production diagnostic — no mocks/stubs).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
# Real production diagnostic across config shapes:
node --import tsx ./harness.mts   # slackDoctor.collectMutableAllowlistWarnings({ cfg })

# Regression suite:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/slack/src/doctor.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** Real console output from `node --import tsx` driving the production `slackDoctor.collectMutableAllowlistWarnings`:
```
A name-keyed / default (allowlist)   -> WARN: channels.slack.channels."example-channel" is keyed by a channel name…
B id-keyed                           -> [] (no warn)
C name-matching-on                   -> [] (no warn)
D multi-account name-keyed           -> WARN: channels.slack.accounts.work.channels."general" is keyed by a channel name…
E wildcard "*"                       -> [] (no warn)
F name-keyed / groupPolicy:open      -> [] (no warn)   # open admits unmatched channels — no silent drop
G name-keyed / explicit allowlist    -> WARN
H name-keyed / groupPolicy:disabled  -> [] (no warn)
```

**Observed result after fix:** The name-keyed channel is flagged with its config path and a re-key hint under allowlist (default and explicit), including per-account scopes (`channels.slack.accounts.work.channels."general"`). Valid ID keys, the wildcard, name-matching-enabled accounts, and open/disabled group policies produce no warning. Routing behavior is unchanged (diagnostic-only).

**What was not tested:** No live Slack workspace / API call — the change is a static config diagnostic; the routing path it describes (`resolveSlackChannelConfig` returning `{allowed:false}` for a name key under allowlist) is unchanged and already covered by existing tests.

## Validation (supplemental)

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/slack/src/doctor.test.ts` → `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 10 passed (10)`
- `tsgo:extensions` and `tsgo:extensions:test` → exit 0. `oxlint` clean. `git diff --check` clean.
